questions.question: What is the name of the scheme recently approved to provide assured treatment to road accident victims?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister launched PM RAHAT Road Accident Victim Hospitalization and Assured Treatment Scheme from Seva Teerth to ensure immediate medical help for accident victims. The scheme aims to prevent avoidable deaths, as nearly 50 percent of road accident deaths can be reduced with treatment in the Golden Hour. It is linked with Emergency Response Support System 112 helpline for quick ambulance and hospital coordination. Every eligible victim will get cashless treatment up to ₹1.5 lakh for 7 days from the date of accident. The scheme integrates Electronic Detailed Accident Report platform of Ministry of Road Transport and Highways with Transaction Management System 2.0 of National Health Authority.

questions.question: Which state has become the first in India to adopt a comprehensive Urban Policy?
questions.explanation: Kerala became India’s first state to adopt a comprehensive Urban Policy for the next 25 years. The policy was announced in the 2023–24 State Budget and approved by the State Cabinet. Kerala Urban Policy Commission was formed in December 2023 and submitted its report in March 2025. A global conference in Kochi discussed the report and suggestions were included in the final policy. The Commission estimates 80 percent of Kerala will be urbanised by 2050. The policy promotes climate-smart cities, scientific planning, and decentralised governance. It focuses on reducing inequalities, ensuring social support, and improving urban amenities. It suggests Public Private People Partnership for future development.

questions.question: Spot-bellied Eagle-Owl, which was recently spotted in Corbett Reserve, is popularly known by which name?
questions.explanation: Spot-bellied Eagle-Owl, called Ghost of the Forest, was recently photographed in Corbett Reserve, Uttarakhand. Scientific name is Ketupa nipalensis and it is a large forest eagle owl. It is found in India, Sri Lanka, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and parts of Southeast Asia. It lives in tropical and subtropical forests, woodlands and savannas. It is one of the longest owls in the world with chocolate brown body and white spots. It is listed as Least Concern in International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red list.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as State Election Commissioner of Jammu and Kashmir by Lieutenant Governor in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir Manoj Sinha appointed Shantmanu, a retired Indian Administrative Service officer, as State Election Commissioner. The appointment is for the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It was made under sub-section 3 of Section 36 of the Jammu and Kashmir Panchayati Raj Act, 1989. He will hold office for five years or until he attains 70 years of age, whichever is earlier. The appointment will be effective from the date he assumes charge. The government will separately notify the detailed terms and conditions of his service.

questions.question: Which country announced its withdrawal from the Ottawa Convention (Mine Ban Treaty) in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Poland announced withdrawal from the Ottawa Convention to allow quick deployment of Anti-Personnel Landmines (APLs) along its eastern borders if needed. The Ottawa Convention, also called the Mine Ban Treaty, bans use, production, stockpiling and transfer of APLs. Member countries must destroy stockpiled APLs within 4 years and clear planted mines within 10 years. Extension of up to 10 years can be requested for mine destruction. Countries must submit annual reports on stockpiles, mine locations and destruction progress. The treaty came into force on 1 March 1999 and has unlimited duration.

questions.question: Which city is the venue for the National Round Table Conference of ECI and State Election Commissioners (SECs)?
questions.explanation: Election Commission of India (ECI) will hold National Round Table Conference with State Election Commissioners (SECs) on 24 February 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi after 27 years; last held in 1999. It will be chaired by Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar with Election Commissioners and participation of all States and Union Territories. The objective is to improve coordination between ECI and SECs within their legal frameworks and promote cooperative federalism. Discussions will cover sharing of technology, Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) and Electoral Rolls.

questions.question: Panama disease, which was recently in the news, primarily affects which crop?
questions.explanation: Scientists discovered a gene that may protect bananas from Panama disease. Panama disease is also called Fusarium wilt. It is caused by fungus Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense living in soil for many years. It mainly affects Cavendish or G9 banana cultivar, most widely grown variety. Fungus infects through roots and blocks water and nutrient transport. Due to severe impact, it is called banana cancer. Symptoms include yellowing of older leaves, turning brown and dry. Stem becomes dark brown and plant finally dies. Dead leaves hang around lower stem forming skirt-like structure.

questions.question: Kole Wetland, that was recently seen in news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: A recent study at Kole Wetlands recorded 12 species of pygmy grasshoppers. It is located in Kerala and is a Ramsar site since 2002. The name ‘Kole’ means “bumper crop” in Malayalam due to high productivity. It is one of the largest, most productive and threatened wetlands in Kerala. The area is low-lying with a narrow central strip and cultivated pockets. It faces seasonal saltwater ingression and monsoon submergence.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book Atal Bihari Vajpayee: The Eternal Statesman released by Vice-President C. P. Radhakrishnan in February 2026?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan released the book Atal Bihari Vajpayee: The Eternal Statesman. The book is authored by Vijay Goel. It was launched at Dr Ambedkar International Centre on February 21. The Vice-President called it a tribute to Atal Bihari Vajpayee, Bharat Ratna awardee. Vajpayee was praised for integrity, inclusive politics and democratic values.

questions.question: What is ‘Contarinia icardiflores’ that was recently seen in news?

options.label: D
options.text: Newly discovered species of blossom midge

answer.label: D
answer.text: Newly discovered species of blossom midge

questions.explanation: Scientists from the ICAR-Directorate of Floricultural Research have discovered a new species of blossom midge named Contarinia icardiflores. Contarinia icardiflores is a newly identified species belonging to the genus Contarinia, whose members are small insects recognized as serious pests of ornamental and food crops worldwide. The larvae of this species feed inside flower buds or blossoms, often leading to severe yield losses in agricultural and horticultural crops. The species has been named in honour of ICAR-DFR to acknowledge the institute’s significant contributions to floriculture research.
